channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, star of ‘South Pacific,’ dies at 93
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film of "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI and BOB THOMAS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Harris to address Al Smith charity dinner by video, organizers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will address the Al Smith charity dinner by video Thursday night, after deciding to skip the event in person in a break with presidential campaign tradition, organizers said. Former President Donald Trump is planning to attend in person.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Liam Payne, former One Direction member, dies at 31 in Argentina hotel fall
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Former One Direction singer Liam Payne, 31, whose chart-topping British boy band generated a global following of swooning fans, was found dead Wednesday after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Sextortion occurs when a victim is coerced to provide sexually explicit photos or videos of themselves and threatened with them being shared publicly.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of fire pits recalled over possible 'flame jetting'
item.description: Thousands of fire pits are being recalled due to "flame jetting and fire spreading" hazards that have resulted in over a dozen burn injuries, according to the U.S. Consumer Protection Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KREX 5

item.title: Universal will open fourth Orlando theme park next May
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Universal Orlando Resort will open its fourth theme park in central Florida just in time for Memorial Day next year, upping the competition against Disney in the theme park capital of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Drug kingpin Demetrius 'Big Meech' Flenory leaves federal prison for a residential program in Miami
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Demetrius “Big Meech” Flenory, who co-founded the notorious drug trafficking organization Black Mafia Family, will serve the remainder of his prison sentence in a residential reentry program in Miami, according to the Federal Bureau of Prisons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: BRADENTON BEACH, Fla. (AP) — When a hurricane sets its sights on Florida, storm-weary residents may think of catastrophic wind, hammering rain and dangerous storm surge. Mounds of sand swallowing their homes? Not so much.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama to execute man for killing 5 in what he says was a meth-fueled rampage
item.description: Alabama prepared Thursday to put to death a man who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals to allow his execution to go forward. Derrick Dearman, 36, is scheduled to be executed by lethal injection at 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wild cat native to Asia and Africa caught in Chicago suburbs: 'Can kill a small child'
item.description: Caracals, an internationally protected species, are native to Africa, the Middle East and India, per the Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Medina, KRON4

item.title: Jane Fonda to receive lifetime achievement award from actors’ guild
item.description: Actor and humanitarian Jane Fonda is adding the SAG life achievement award to her many accolades. The 86-year-old will be given the prize at the Screen Actors Guild Awards in February, the guild said Thursday. Fonda said in a statement that she was “deeply honored and humbled” that she was chosen.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar may be dead
item.description: The Israeli military said Thursday it was looking into whether Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in a military operation in Gaza. Authorities were conducting DNA tests on a body to determine if it is him, an Israeli security official said.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Who is Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar?
item.description: Israel says it is looking into the possibility that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, a mastermind of the Oct. 7, 2023, attack that triggered the war in Gaza, has been killed in a military operation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Friends and musicians express heartbreak at the death of singer Liam Payne at age 31
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Friends, collaborators and fellow musicians expressed shock and sadness Thursday over the death of former One Direction singer Liam Payne, who died in Buenos Aires, Argentina Wednesday at age 31. A look at some of the reactions: __ "We are heartbroken.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officials ask for help in locating missing 7-year-old girl
item.description: Authorities have sent out an advisory asking the public for help in locating a missing seven-year-old girl near Hill Air Force Base.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Funeral home owner pleads guilty to assaulting police and journalists during Capitol riot
item.description: A Long Island funeral home owner has pleaded guilty to spraying wasp killer at police officers and assaulting two journalists, including an Associated Press photographer, during a mob’s riot at the U.S. Capitol nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After hurricane, with no running water, residents organize to meet a basic need
item.description: It takes water to flush a toilet yet tens of thousands of North Carolinians have been without it since Hurricane Helene ripped through the state three weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: The Biden administration has now canceled loans for more than 1 million public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez family: We suspected abuse from the beginning
item.description: Karen VanderMolen-Copley said the family "knew there was abuse at gut level," but it wasn't something they wanted to believe.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions, but the measures are unlikely to dramatically change access — at least not immediately.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to pick ‘best’ Republicans for 2nd term Cabinet: Lutnick
item.description: Donald Trump is in the unusual position of not being backed by a number of members of his previous Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: The ballot’s mailed but you die before Election Day. Now what?
item.description: Some states allow them to be counted, and some don't. But most states have never addressed the question.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Whistleblowers claim Border Patrol surveillance cameras 'out of service' as GOP demands answers from DHS
item.description: Whistleblowers have come forward about an alarming issue at the southern border claiming that crucial remote surveillance cameras in multiple sectors have not been operational.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of Erik and Lyle Menendez call for their release and say they're victims who were vilified
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Multiple generations of family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez pleaded for the brothers' release from prison on Wednesday, saying they deserve to be free despite life sentences for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills because they had been “brutalized” and sexuall
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officer involved in shooting at Arizona port of entry, US Customs and Border Protection says
item.description: A person was injured in an officer-involved shooting on Wednesday at the Deconcini Port of Entry in Nogales, Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 dead, several injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and several others were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart has a ton of great deals on coveted LEGO sets — Disney, Harry Potter and more
item.description: Adults and kids alike love LEGO, but they're usually pretty pricey. Right now, however, LEGO sets are on sale at Walmart.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Google Trends 'Frightgeist' lists the most popular Halloween costumes for 2024
item.description: Whether you are unsure about what your Halloween costume should be or want to stand out from the crowd, Google has you covered.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina called on others 'to join'
item.description: The man says he believed workers were withholding supplies, but soon discovered that wasn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall: 'The height of blasphemy'
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Tom Donohue, former longtime Chamber of Commerce CEO, dies
item.description: Tom Donohue, who led the U.S. Chamber of Commerce for more than two decades before retiring in 2021, has died, the business lobbying giant announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, WGN 9
